items.title: Waarom Slot lang wakker lag richting FA Cup-duel met Brighton
items.summary: Arne Slot is er nog niet uit of hij zaterdag spelers rust gaat geven in het FA Cup-duel met Brighton & Hove Albion. De Nederlandse trainer wil graag verder komen met Liverpool, maar moet ook rekening houden met de belasting van zijn spelers.

items.title: Tottenham manager latest, Ratcliffe reaction, FA Cup fourth round and more – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news, previews and updates before the weekend ⚽ Ten things to look out for in the FA Cup | And email Niall Brentford striker Igor Thiago has signed a new contract, extending his deal until 2031, with the option for an additional 12 months. The Brazilian was signed from Club Brugge in February 2024, but had to overcome a knee injury last season before hitting form this term, scoring 17 Premier League goals so far. “I love the club and the people in the club,” said Thiago. “It’s a true love, a real love. When the fans support me, and I see them singing my name and singing my song, it gives me more power. It’s been a great season for us. Everybody has been on the same page. I hope we can get something special from it.” Continue reading...

items.title: Zimbabwe stun Australia at T20 World Cup
items.summary: Zimbabwe beat Australia by 23 runs at the T20 Cricket World Cup on Friday in Colombo.

items.title: Sterling had wat te kiezen: 'Hij gelooft écht in opvattingen van Van Persie'
items.summary: Feyenoord verraste vriend en vijand met de aankondiging van Raheem Sterling . De 82-voudig Engels international zat zonder club, maar maakt dit seizoen af in Rotterdam. Rob Aarts is van mening dat zonder Van Persie deze transfer niet tot stand was gekomen.

items.title: Nieuwe realiteit bij PSV: geen stress en meer rust, vooral voor Saibari
items.summary: Na maanden vol voetbalgeweld op alle fronten is PSV nu in een fase van het seizoen aanbeland met meer rust en minder spanning. Dat laatste is natuurlijk ingegeven door de megavoorsprong in de Eredivisie. Niemand die zich dus zorgen maakt dat de derde keeper op doel moet of sterspeler Ismael Saibari even vrijaf krijgt en dus tegen FC Volendam ontbreekt.

items.title: Ajax-directeur Geelen treedt toe tot belangrijk adviesorgaan UEFA
items.summary: Ajax-directeur Menno Geelen treedt toe tot het comité van de UEFA dat meedenkt over de Europese clubcompetities. De 44-jarige bestuurder gaat in die rol onder meer de belangen van het Nederlandse clubvoetbal behartigen.

items.title: VI Live: PSV zet jacht op extreem vroege titel voort tegen FC Volendam
items.summary: PSV gaat vrijdagavond op bezoek bij FC Volendam. De Eindhovenaren willen de grote voorsprong weer wat uitbreiden met het oog op een extreem vroege landstitel. Volg de wedstrijd vanaf 20.00 uur op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Hull's 'complete nobody' out to stun Chelsea on Rosenior return
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at Hull City boss Sergej Jakirovic and how the unheralded appointment has them challenging for promotion to the Premier League.

items.title: 41 and thriving! | LeBron becomes oldest NBA player to record triple-double!
items.summary: Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James recorded 28 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds in his team's win over the Dallas Mavericks, becoming the oldest NBA player to score a triple-double.

items.title: ‘He would fill you with confidence’: how Liam Rosenior made his mark at Hull
items.summary: Chelsea manager, back at Hull in the Cup on Friday, made a strong impression on Humberside, as ex-colleagues explain “That’s the best message I’ve received,” Liam Rosenior wrote, accompanied by laughing emojis, in response to one of the many congratulation posts sent after his surprise appointment as the Chelsea head coach. His former Hull teammate James Chester had wickedly told him: “After my time with you at Derby I never thought you’d have been Chelsea manager,” alongside a photograph of Chester in the pub with his fellow former Tigers Robbie Brady and Paul McShane, raising a glass to Rosenior. It was a sign of the close bonds Rosenior built at Hull as a player and head coach. He returns as a Champions League head coach on Friday in the FA Cup, looking to avoid an upset against a team aiming to make it a more regular rivalry. Rosenior spent seven seasons on Humberside, five as a player and two in the dugout, which were crucial for his development. Continue reading...

items.title: Northern Ireland boss O'Neill set to take over at Blackburn
items.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill is set to join Championship club Blackburn, initially in a joint role until the end of the season.

items.title: Ireland captain Stirling out of T20 World Cup
items.summary: Ireland captain Paul Stirling has been ruled out of the rest of the T20 World Cup because of a knee injury.

items.title: Is Price back to his best? 'There's no way I can't pick up a major'
items.summary: Gerwyn Price has warned his rivals he is back playing some of the best darts of his career and says a major title is just around the corner after winning Night Two of the Premier League in Antwerp.

items.title: Rice tells Arsenal to ignore noise after PL lead cut
items.summary: Declan Rice has urged Arsenal to "block out the outside noise" but insisted his teammates remain calm after missing the opportunity to move six points clear.

items.title: Barcelona taught 'lesson' by Atlético in Copa rout
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his side were taught a lesson by Atlético Madrid as they suffered a 4-0 loss at the Metropolitano on Thursday in the first leg of the Copa del Rey semifinal between the two teams.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to Man Utd job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: 'Incredible' Álvarez leads Atleti thrashing of Barça
items.summary: Diego Simeone said Julián Álvarez's goal and assist in Atlético Madrid's 4-0 Copa del Rey thrashing of Barcelona will help the forward reset after ending an 11-game scoring drought.

items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Will Arsenal's end-of-season form haunt them again?
items.summary: Arsenal are four points clear at the top of the Premier League with 12 games to play - but will recent history repeat itself?

items.title: FA Cup fourth round: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Burnley have the chance of a Cup run, Leicester fear an unwelcome repeat and Brighton fans get a raw deal Chelsea have kept two clean sheets in 10 games since appointing Liam Rosenior as head coach last month. Repeated doziness at the back has cost them. They have held commanding advantages against Charlton, Crystal Palace, Wolves and Leeds, only to give away silly goals. It is a bad habit and proved costly when a 2-0 lead was squandered during Tuesday’s draw with Leeds. Rosenior was livid afterwards, and is waiting for a consistent performance. Chelsea travel to Hull , Rosenior’s former club, on Friday night. They will surely advance against Championship opponents, but how they do it will matter. It is time for them to get serious. Jacob Steinberg Hull City v Chelsea, Friday 7.45pm (all times GMT) Burton Albion v West Ham, Saturday 12.15pm Burnley v Mansfield, Saturday 3pm Southampton v Leicester, Saturday 3pm Continue reading...
